keyPoints: Demand paging is a technique used in virtual memory management to reduce the overhead of page faults by preloading frequently used pages into memory.
Copy-on-write (COW) is a technique used to minimize the overhead of write operations by creating a copy of the page only when it is modified.
A page table is a data structure used to manage the mapping between virtual page numbers and physical page frames in a computer system.
A page fault is an event that occurs when a program attempts to access a page that is not in physical memory.
The page replacement algorithm is used to determine which page to replace when the system runs out of physical memory.
The Least Recently Used (LRU) algorithm is a popular page replacement algorithm that replaces the page that has not been accessed for the longest time.
The First-In-First-Out (FIFO) algorithm is another page replacement algorithm that replaces the page that was brought into memory first.
The Optimal Page Replacement (OPR) algorithm is a page replacement algorithm that determines the optimal page to replace based on the page's access history.
The Second-Chance (SC) algorithm is a page replacement algorithm that uses a combination of LRU and FIFO to replace the page that was brought into memory first.
The Clock algorithm is a page replacement algorithm that uses a circular list to keep track of the order of page accesses.
